output.summary: Move dynamic-content-carousel-scoped styles from swiper.css into a new block-specific CSS file (DynamicContentCarousel.css). Consolidate the duplicated card image hover zoom effect (currently in both swiper.css and Archive.css) into a shared global location. Add a theme-wide prefers-reduced-motion rule in body.css so all transitions/animations across the theme respect user preferences, instead of only the swiper module.

blockSpecific.purpose: Equal-height card chain — ensures cards stretch to fill swiper slide
blockSpecific.verdict: MOVE to DynamicContentCarousel.css — truly block-specific carousel layout

duplicatedCardHoverZoom.verdict: REMOVE from both locations — make generic on .card-post and .card-team-member directly
REMOVE from both locations — make generic

reducedMotionRules.verdict: REMOVE — will be covered by theme-wide reduced-motion rule

sharedSwiperStyles.description: CSS custom properties (:root), .swiper-notification, .swiper-controls hiding logic, progress bar gradient, .carousel-nav-btn, .modal-close-btn, @keyframes carousel-nav-gradient-sweep, and generic swiper prefers-reduced-motion rules
sharedSwiperStyles.verdict: KEEP in swiper.css — these are shared swiper infrastructure used by multiple carousel blocks

cardTemplatesAnalysis.cardPost: parts/card-post.php — has overflow-hidden container (line 23 div.overflow-hidden) wrapping the image. Hover zoom will work if applied to .card-post img globally.
cardTemplatesAnalysis.cardTeamMember: parts/card-team-member.php — has overflow-hidden container (line 22 div.overflow-hidden) wrapping the image. Hover zoom will work if applied to .card-team-member img globally.
cardTemplatesAnalysis.cardResource: parts/card-resource.php — NO image element, only text and taxonomy pills. Hover effect is bg-color change via Tailwind classes.
cardTemplatesAnalysis.cardPolicy: parts/card-policy.php — NO image element, only text and taxonomy pills. Hover effect is bg-color change via Tailwind classes.
cardTemplatesAnalysis.conclusion: Only card-post and card-team-member have images with overflow-hidden containers. These are the only two that need hover zoom.

reducedMotionAnalysis.currentState: prefers-reduced-motion is ONLY handled in swiper.css (lines 163-182). No theme-wide rule exists.
gaps: body.css has scroll-behavior: smooth (line 26) with NO reduced-motion override
body.css has body transition: background-color 0.3s (line 48) with NO reduced-motion override
Archive.css card hover transitions (.archive .card-resource article, .archive .card-policy article) have NO reduced-motion handling
Archive.css card image hover zoom (.archive .card-team-member img, .archive .card-post img) has NO reduced-motion handling
reducedMotionAnalysis.recommendation: Add a theme-wide @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule in body.css that blanket-disables transitions and animations (similar to Tailwind's motion-reduce utilities). This replaces per-component reduced-motion overrides and ensures no future CSS additions miss this.

blockCssPattern.precedent: blocks/Archive/Archive.css is the only existing block with its own CSS file
blockCssPattern.bundling: webpack.config.js line 33: glob.sync('./blocks/**/*.css') auto-includes any CSS file in any block folder into the screen.css bundle
blockCssPattern.convention: Name the file blocks/DynamicContentCarousel/DynamicContentCarousel.css to match the Archive pattern

fileChanges.description: Create block-specific CSS file for DynamicContentCarousel
fileChanges.details: Contains only the truly block-specific style: .dynamic-content-carousel .swiper-slide > * { height: 100%; } for equal-height card chain within the carousel. This is the only rule that is unique to this block's carousel layout and cannot be generalized. Auto-bundled by webpack via glob.sync('./blocks/**/*.css').

Remove lines 147-181: the equal-height card chain rule (moves to DynamicContentCarousel.css), the card image hover zoom rules (moving to body.css as generic card styles), and the block-scoped reduced-motion rules for card images (replaced by theme-wide rule). Keep all shared swiper infrastructure intact (CSS vars, .swiper-notification, .swiper-controls, progress bar, .carousel-nav-btn, .modal-close-btn, @keyframes, and the generic swiper reduced-motion rules for .swiper-wrapper, .carousel-nav-btn::before, .modal-close-btn::before, .swiper-pagination-progressbar).

fileChanges.description: Add theme-wide prefers-reduced-motion rule and generic card hover zoom styles
fileChanges.details: 1) Add a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block that: (a) sets html { scroll-behavior: auto; }, (b) applies a broad *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } rule. This is the standard approach (similar to CSS reset patterns) that ensures all animations and transitions respect user preference without needing per-component overrides. 2) Add generic card image hover zoom styles (not scoped to any block): .card-post img, .card-team-member img { transition: transform 0.3s ease; } and .card-post:hover img, .card-team-member:hover img { transform: scale(1.05); } — these work everywhere cards are used. Both will automatically respect reduced-motion via the blanket rule above.

fileChanges.description: Remove duplicated card image hover zoom styles
fileChanges.details: Remove lines 133-142: the .archive .card-team-member img, .archive .card-post img transition and hover scale rules. These are now handled generically in body.css without needing block-scoped selectors. The Archive-specific styles (alternating layout, dividers, filter button, resource/policy card transitions, blog card CTA hover) remain.

After the theme-wide reduced-motion rule is added in body.css, the per-component reduced-motion overrides in swiper.css (lines 163-182) become redundant for most rules. However, the !important overrides on .swiper-wrapper { transition-duration: 0ms !important; } are needed because Swiper JS sets inline styles. Keep ONLY the .swiper-wrapper transition-duration override (which fights inline styles), and the .swiper-pagination-progressbar-fill transition override (also fighting inline styles). Remove the .carousel-nav-btn::before, .modal-close-btn::before, and .dynamic-content-carousel card image overrides — the theme-wide rule handles these.

designDecisions.question: Should the card image hover zoom be generic (applied to .card-post and .card-team-member globally) or scoped per-block?
designDecisions.recommendation: Make it generic. Both Archive and DynamicContentCarousel apply identical hover zoom (scale 1.05, 0.3s ease). The card templates already have overflow-hidden containers. Any context where these cards appear should get the zoom effect. This eliminates duplication and ensures future blocks using these cards automatically get the hover zoom.
designDecisions.evidence: Archive.css lines 133-142 and swiper.css lines 151-160 are identical. card-post.php line 23 and card-team-member.php line 22 both have overflow-hidden wrappers.

designDecisions.question: Should prefers-reduced-motion be handled theme-wide or per-component?
designDecisions.recommendation: Theme-wide. The current approach of only handling it in swiper.css leaves gaps: body.css smooth scrolling, Archive.css transitions, and any future CSS additions. A blanket rule in body.css (animation-duration: 0.01ms, transition-duration: 0.01ms) is the industry standard approach used by modern CSS resets. The 0.01ms value (not 0ms) ensures JavaScript transitionend/animationend events still fire. Swiper's inline-style overrides still need specific !important rules since they bypass CSS specificity.
designDecisions.evidence: Only swiper.css has prefers-reduced-motion handling. body.css scroll-behavior: smooth and Archive.css transition rules have no reduced-motion overrides. MDN and WCAG 2.1 SC 2.3.3 recommend respecting this preference globally.

designDecisions.question: Where should block-specific CSS live for DynamicContentCarousel?
designDecisions.recommendation: Create blocks/DynamicContentCarousel/DynamicContentCarousel.css following the Archive block precedent. Webpack auto-bundles via glob.sync('./blocks/**/*.css'). No PHP changes needed.
designDecisions.evidence: blocks/Archive/Archive.css exists as the established pattern. webpack.config.js line 33 auto-includes block CSS files.

output.qaReason: CSS refactoring changes selectors and specificity. Need to verify: (1) card hover zoom works in both DynamicContentCarousel and Archive, (2) carousel equal-height layout still works, (3) prefers-reduced-motion disables all animations, (4) Swiper navigation/pagination animations still work in normal mode.
